headline: MiniMax M2.7 Brings Agentic Self-Evolution to Open Source
lede: The latest compact model bridges the gap between frontier capabilities and local accessibility. By utilizing iterative self-correction, it offers a distinct approach to autonomous task execution.

headline: Pat Gelsinger on the New Trinity of Computing
lede: The former Intel CEO discusses the convergence of classical, AI, and quantum architectures. He argues that future infrastructure requires a fundamental shift in how we approach inference efficiency.
pullquote: The next era of hardware success depends on integrating classical, AI, and quantum systems into a unified trinity of compute.

headline: Reversible Logic and the Toffoli Gate Foundation
lede: Reversible computing offers a theoretical path toward bypassing the energy dissipation limits defined by Landauer’s principle. The Toffoli gate serves as the modular building block for these energy-efficient Boolean operations.

headline: Turning Smartphones Into Mobile Physics Laboratories
lede: By tapping into integrated sensors, this toolkit transforms everyday mobile devices into precision measurement instruments. It provides a robust framework for documenting physical phenomena from Doppler shifts to rotation speeds.

headline: iOS Bug Locks Users Out with Czech Characters
lede: A recent software update has rendered devices inaccessible by refusing to accept specific alphanumeric characters during passcode entry. The incident highlights a critical failure in maintaining input compatibility for legacy credentials.

headline: The Miller Principle and the Erosion of Reading
lede: Nearly two decades ago, the Miller Principle posited that written documentation is largely ignored by the intended audience. In an era of AI-generated content, this observation remains a defining challenge for technical communicators.

headline: Gaming the System: AI Benchmarks Face Scrutiny
lede: Researchers demonstrate that prominent AI benchmarks are highly susceptible to environment manipulation and prompt injection. Current evaluation frameworks often reward gaming the infrastructure rather than achieving genuine model capability.

headline: The Rebranding of Eleventy into Build Awesome
lede: The transition of the static site generator Eleventy into a commercialized product signals a definitive shift in the web development lifecycle. Long-time contributors now navigate the cooling of a once-community-led ecosystem.

headline: Appeals Court Overturns Century-Old Distilling Ban
lede: The Fifth Circuit has declared the federal prohibition on home distilling unconstitutional, ending a 158-year-old restriction. The court found that total prohibition provides no legitimate basis for congressional tax enforcement.

headline: Small Models Match Frontier Vulnerability Detection
lede: Recent testing confirms that vulnerability discovery does not require massive parameter counts. Success rests on the architecture of the automation pipeline and the orchestration of modular system checks.
